title: [InstCombine]: Handle known negative variables with KnownBits
summary:
The developers generalized the "large divisor" optimization from handling only constant negative values to also supporting dynamic values known to be negative via `KnownBits` analysis. The original pattern transformed `Op0 / C` → `zext(Op0 >= C)` when `C` is a constant negative. The patch adds a parallel transform using `computeKnownBits` to catch cases where the divisor is proven negative at compile time, even if not constant.

This demonstrates a key InstCombine generalization strategy: when a pattern works for constants, check if equivalent known-bits information can enable the same transform for non-constants. The approach preserves the core logic while expanding coverage through static analysis. Developers should look for similar opportunities where value guarantees (like sign bits) from `KnownBits`, `ValueTracking`, or pattern matching can unlock existing optimizations for broader cases.
